title: Silky Spicy Malaysian Tofu Bao
description: A flavorful, silky tofu filling with spicy sambal, wrapped in soft bao buns, perfect for meal prep.
instructions: In a wok, heat 1 tbsp peanut oil over medium heat. Add the firm tofu cubes and stir‑fry until golden brown, about 5 minutes. Remove tofu and set aside.
Add the remaining peanut oil to the wok. Stir in the silky tofu cubes and fry for 2 minutes, then add the red chili paste and soy sauce. Toss to coat evenly and cook for another 2 minutes.
Whisk the rice flour with the water until smooth, then pour the mixture into the wok. Stir constantly to form a thick, silky sauce. Let it simmer for 3 minutes until it thickens slightly.
Fold in the cooked tofu and add a splash of lime juice, then remove from heat. Let the mixture cool before filling the bao buns.
Assemble by placing a spoonful of tofu filling into each warm bao bun, topping with pickled red cabbage, chopped cilantro, and a sprinkle of sesame seeds. Serve immediately or pack for meal prep.
